text:
<think>I should start by calculating how many games Steve starts with, which is the console plus 5 games for his birthday.</think>
Steve starts with 5 games for his birthday.
<think>Now, I need to determine how many games he buys in the first year, which is 1 game per month for 12 months.</think>
In the first year, he buys 1 game per month for 12 months, totaling 12 games.
<think>I should also remember to add the 5 games he receives for Christmas in the first year.</think>
He also receives 5 games for Christmas in the first year.
<think>Next, I need to calculate how many games he buys in the second year, which is 2 games per month for 12 months.</think>
In the second year, he buys 2 games per month for 12 months, totaling 24 games.
<think>I should add the 5 games he receives for Christmas in the second year as well.</think>
He receives another 5 games for Christmas in the second year.
<think>Now, I need to calculate how many games he buys in the third year, which is 4 games per month for 12 months.</think>
In the third year, he buys 4 games per month for 12 months, totaling 48 games.
<think>I should also add the 5 games he receives for Christmas in the third year.</think>
He receives another 5 games for Christmas in the third year.
<think>Now, I need to sum all the games: 5 (birthday) + 12 (year 1) + 5 (Christmas 1) + 24 (year 2) + 5 (Christmas 2) + 48 (year 3) + 5 (Christmas 3).</think>
Adding these together: 5 + 12 + 5 + 24 + 5 + 48 + 5 = 104.
<think>I should now present the final answer after the required ####.</think>
#### 104
